title: Cross-section data for the reactions 13C(3He,p0)15N to 13C(3He,p15)15N and 13C(3He,d0)14N at energies up to 6 MeV

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.555, p.165454 (2024)

x4freetext: The targets are composed of a 110-120 nm thick layer
bulk substrates made of chromium and silicon, with a
thickness of ~400 um and dimensions of 10 mm x 15 mm.
A 3-5 nm thick gold was applied on top as a reference
The silicon surface barrier detector with a
depletion depth of 2000 um, a solid angle of
30.3 +/-1.2 msr, and an energy resolution of 22 keV at
135 degree. A 5 um Ni foil and a 12 um Mylar foil
placed in front of detector to stop backscattered
detector with a depletion depth of 2000 um, a solid
angle of 77.5 +/- 3.0 msr, and an energy resolution
of 40 keV. At 175 degrees, an annular lithium-drifted
silicon detector of 2000 um, a solid angle of
19.5+/- 0.8 msr and an energy resolution of 30 keV.
A 50 um Mylar foil placed in front of detector to block
The main source for overall uncertainty are:
The ratio of areal densities of gold
